nodes.en: Cryptographic protection of data confidentiality and integrity during storage and transmission—spanning symmetric ciphers (AES, ChaCha20) for shared-key encryption and asymmetric methods (RSA, ECDSA) for key exchange and authentication. Distinct from compression by prioritizing secrecy and tamper-detection over size reduction.
nodes.cs: Kryptografická ochrana důvěrnosti a integrity dat během ukládání a přenosu—zahrnující symetrické šifry (AES, ChaCha20) pro sdílené klíče a asymetrické metody (RSA, ECDSA) pro výměnu klíčů a autentifikaci. Odlišuje se od komprese upřednostňováním tajnosti a detekce manipulace před redukcí velikosti.
nodes.brief:
Encryption and security cover the cryptographic protection of data confidentiality and integrity during storage and transmission, spanning symmetric ciphers such as AES and ChaCha20 for shared-key encryption and asymmetric methods such as RSA and ECDSA for key exchange and authentication.

The branch is distinct from [[11.02]] Compression Methods by prioritizing secrecy and tamper-detection over size reduction, though the two are frequently applied in sequence — compress, then encrypt — since encrypted data is incompressible. It intersects [[11.04]] Backup Strategies wherever an archive's redundancy copies must themselves be encrypted at rest, and it creates the long-horizon risk [[11.05]] Data Recovery exists to answer: an encryption key lost alongside its data renders that data permanently, rather than temporarily, inaccessible.

The [NIST Cryptographic Standards and Guidelines](https://csrc.nist.gov/projects/cryptographic-standards-and-guidelines) catalog the vetted algorithms referenced above, and the [Digital Preservation Coalition Handbook](https://www.dpconline.org/handbook) discusses key-management practice specific to long-term archival custody.
